perturbation.original_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a final definitive agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) neither Captaris nor any of’ its Representatives make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such final definitive agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a preliminary, non-binding agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) neither Captaris nor any of’ its Representatives make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such preliminary, non-binding agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'final definitive agreement' to 'preliminary, non-binding agreement'. This introduces an contradiction. The original clause states that only warranties made in a 'final definitive agreement' have legal effect. By changing this to 'preliminary, non-binding agreement', it implies that even a non-binding agreement could create legal obligations, contradicting the very nature of a non-binding agreement. This creates a legal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a preliminary, non-binding agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) neither Captaris nor any of’ its Representatives make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such preliminary, non-binding agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that only warranties made in a 'final definitive agreement' have legal effect. The changed text replaces 'final definitive agreement' with 'preliminary, non-binding agreement'. This implies that even a non-binding agreement could create legal obligations. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and thus it implies that preliminary, non-binding agreements create legal obligations, which contradicts the nature of a non-binding agreement. This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
